meta.name: Asian-Fusion Noodle Stir-Fry
meta.subtitle: Fast noodles with crisp-tender vegetables and a soy-ginger-garlic sauce (mix-and-match protein).

source.name: SSA Canonical Catalog
notes: Designed for SSA planners: weeknight-ready, highly adaptable, works with pantry noodles and mixed vegetables.
Sauce uses an optional cornstarch slurry for cling; can be made gluten-free with tamari + GF noodles.

summary: A flexible noodle stir-fry with vegetables and a quick soy-ginger-garlic sauce. Choose your noodles, choose your veg, add optional protein, and finish with sesame and citrus.

optional.label: Pan lid
optional.notes: Helpful for a short steam if using dense vegetables (e.g., broccoli stems).

notes: Use tamari + gluten-free noodles for gluten-free.
If using oyster sauce or fish sauce, note those may contain shellfish/fish.

ingredients.label: Noodles (lo mein, ramen, rice noodles, or spaghetti in a pinch)
notes: For rice noodles, soak/cook per package and rinse briefly to stop cooking.
For ramen bricks, discard seasoning packet and cook noodles separately.

ingredients.label: Cabbage, thinly sliced
notes: Napa, green cabbage, or a bag of coleslaw mix works well.

ingredients.label: Honey, brown sugar, or maple syrup
notes: Optional; balances salt/acid. Reduce or omit for low-sugar.

ingredients.label: Optional cooked protein (chicken, shrimp, tofu, beef, or eggs)
notes: If protein is raw, cook it first in the pan, remove, then proceed with vegetables and add back at the end.

ingredients.label: Red pepper flakes or chili crisp (optional)

steps.title: Cook noodles and set aside
instructions: Bring a pot of water to a boil.
Cook noodles according to package directions until just tender (al dente).
Drain and rinse briefly if needed (especially for rice noodles) to prevent sticking.
Toss with a tiny drizzle of neutral oil if your noodles tend to clump.
checks: Noodles should be just tender; they will cook a little more in the pan.

steps.title: Mix sauce
instructions: In a bowl, whisk soy sauce, rice vinegar, and water/broth.
Add sweetener if using.
If using cornstarch, whisk it in until fully dissolved (no lumps).
Set aside; re-whisk right before pouring (cornstarch settles).
checks: Sauce should be smooth; no visible cornstarch clumps if using it.

steps.title: Heat pan and bloom aromatics
instructions: Heat a large skillet or wok over medium-high heat until hot.
Add neutral oil.
Add garlic and ginger; stir constantly 15–25 seconds until fragrant (avoid browning).
checks: Aromatics should be fragrant, not browned.

steps.title: Stir-fry vegetables
instructions: Add dense vegetables first (broccoli, carrots). Stir-fry 2 minutes.
Add remaining vegetables (peppers, snap peas/beans, mushrooms, cabbage). Stir-fry 2–3 minutes until crisp-tender.
If your pan is overcrowded and vegetables are steaming, cook in two rounds for better texture.
checks: Vegetables should be bright and crisp-tender, not soft.

steps.title: Add noodles and glaze with sauce
instructions: Add cooked noodles to the pan and toss with vegetables.
Re-whisk sauce and pour around the edges of the pan.
Toss continuously for 60–90 seconds until sauce lightly thickens and coats noodles/veg.
If using cooked protein, add it now and toss to warm through.
checks: Sauce should coat noodles lightly. If too thick, add 1–2 tbsp water; if too thin, cook 30 seconds longer.

steps.title: Finish and serve
instructions: Turn off heat and drizzle toasted sesame oil (if using).
Taste and adjust: add a squeeze of citrus for brightness or a pinch of salt if needed.
Top with scallions, sesame seeds, and optional chili.
Serve immediately.

notes: For double batches, stir-fry vegetables in two rounds to keep them crisp.
Sauce scales linearly; keep cornstarch proportional if using.
Noodles absorb sauce over time; refresh with a splash of water when reheating.
Noodles and vegetables tend to soften and weep when thawed.

reheat.skillet: Medium-high, 3–5 minutes, tossing; add 1–2 tbsp water to loosen.
reheat.microwave: Cover; 90 seconds, stir, then 60–90 seconds more with a splash of water.

notes: If cooking raw protein, cook to safe internal temperature before combining with noodles.
Keep pan hot and avoid overcrowding to prevent steaming.

variations.label: Peanut Noodles
variations.notes: Whisk 2–3 tbsp peanut butter into sauce with extra water; finish with lime. Allergen: peanuts.

variations.label: Spicy Sesame
variations.notes: Add 1 tsp chili crisp and 1 tsp sesame oil; reduce sweetener or omit.

variations.label: Orange-Citrus Noodles
variations.notes: Replace half the water with orange juice; finish with lime and a touch more vinegar.

notes: Vegetable target: aim for 4–6 cups vegetables total.
If using spaghetti, cook just shy of al dente and toss immediately in the pan to absorb sauce.
Cornstarch is optional; skipping it yields a lighter, saucier noodle stir-fry.
